## Reference: Scaffolding a New Extension (`azd x init`)

### Choose the audience first

| | **External extension** | **First-party extension** |
|---|---|---|
| Location | Any repo/path | `cli/azd/extensions/<id>/` in `Azure/azure-dev` |
| Command | `azd x init` | `azd x init --internal` |
| Extra files | none | CODEOWNERS entry, lint workflow, release pipeline templates |
| Languages | go, dotnet, javascript, python | **Go only** (currently) |
| Release flow | `azd x release` + `azd x publish` (your repo) | 2-PR flow into azure-dev registries |

Ask the user which one they want if it is not obvious from context.

### Interactive scaffold

```bash
# External (run from your project root)
azd x init

# First-party (run from cli/azd/extensions/)
azd x init --internal
```

`azd x init` collects: **name**, **id**, **namespace**, **language**, **capabilities**, optional
**tags**. It then scaffolds the project, creates a local extension source if needed, builds initial
binaries, packs, publishes to the local source, and installs the extension for immediate use.

### Headless / non-interactive scaffold

Use `--no-prompt` (or the global `--no-prompt`) and supply required flags. Required in headless
mode: `--id`, `--name`, `--capabilities`, and `--language` (unless `--internal`). First-party
(`--internal`) additionally requires `--codeowners`.

```bash
# External, headless
azd x init --no-prompt \
  --id contoso.azd.tagger \
  --name "Resource Tagger" \
  --namespace tagger \
  --language go \
  --capabilities custom-commands,metadata,lifecycle-events \
  --tags azure,tags

# First-party, headless (from cli/azd/extensions/)
azd x init --internal --no-prompt \
  --id microsoft.azd.widget \
  --name "Widget" \
  --namespace widget \
  --capabilities custom-commands,metadata \
  --codeowners "@Azure/azd-team"
```

### `azd x init` flags

| Flag | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `--id` | Extension identifier, dot-separated lowercase segments (e.g. `company.azd.tool`). |
| `--name` | Display name. |
| `--namespace` | Command namespace; dots become nested command groups (`ai.project` → `azd ai project`). |
| `--language` | `go`, `dotnet`, `javascript`, or `python`. |
| `--capabilities` | Comma-separated list (see below). |
| `--tags` | Optional discovery tags (comma-separated / repeatable). |
| `-r, --registry` | Also create a local extension source registry. |
| `--internal` | Scaffold first-party azure-dev files (Go only). Mutually exclusive with `--registry`. |
| `--codeowners` | GitHub handles/teams for the generated CODEOWNERS entry (first-party). |
| `--no-prompt` | Headless mode; requires the flags above. |

### Capabilities (declare in `extension.yaml`)

Exactly these 8 values are valid (`cli/azd/pkg/extensions/registry.go`):

| Capability | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `custom-commands` | Adds new commands under the extension namespace. |
| `lifecycle-events` | Handles project/service events (`preprovision`, `postprovision`, `predeploy`, …) via a `listen` command. |
| `mcp-server` | Exposes Model Context Protocol tools for AI agents. Requires an `mcp` section in the manifest. |
| `service-target-provider` | Provides a custom deploy target (host). |
| `framework-service-provider` | Adds a custom language/framework build+package pipeline. |
| `provisioning-provider` | Provides a custom infrastructure provisioning provider. |
| `validation-provider` | Provides local pre-provision validation checks. |
| `metadata` | Exposes a `metadata` command describing the extension's commands/capabilities. |

Declare only the capabilities you actually implement — the manifest capabilities must match the
code. `service-target-provider` and `provisioning-provider` also require a matching `providers:`
entry.

### The `extension.yaml` manifest

Generated by the scaffold; edit to match your extension. Example (from `microsoft.azd.demo`):

```yaml
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/cli/azd/extensions/extension.schema.json
id: contoso.azd.tagger
namespace: tagger
displayName: Resource Tagger
description: Auto-tag Azure resources and expose tagging tools via MCP.
usage: azd tagger <command> [options]
version: 0.1.0
language: go
capabilities:
  - custom-commands
  - metadata
  - lifecycle-events
# providers:            # required when using *-provider capabilities
#   - name: tagger
#     type: service-target
#     description: Deploys to the tagger target
# examples:             # shown in azd help / registry
#   - name: tag
#     description: Apply a tag to resources
#     usage: azd tagger tag --key team --value platform
```

Key manifest fields: `id`, `namespace`, `displayName`, `description`, `version` (semver),
`language`, `capabilities`, optional `usage`, `providers`, `examples`, `tags`, `requiredAzdVersion`,
and (for mcp-server) an `mcp` section. Validate against `extension.schema.json`.

### Generated project structure (Go)

```
<id>/
├── main.go                 # azdext.Run(cmd.NewRootCommand())
├── extension.yaml          # manifest
├── CHANGELOG.md
├── go.mod / go.sum
├── build.ps1 / build.sh
└── internal/
    └── cmd/
        ├── root.go         # root command + subcommand wiring
        ├── context.go, prompt.go, listen.go, version.go, metadata.go  (samples)
```
